figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of Evo-MedAgent. A tool-augmented LLM agent processes CXR questions sequentially while reading from and writing to a self-evolving memory via test-time learning. Before solving, the agent retrieves relevant episodic cases, procedural rules, and tool governance guidance. After receiving ground-truth feedback, it reflects and refines all three memory stores.

Table 1: Ablation results on ChestAgentBench (tool-free). S = Procedural memory, E = Episodic memory. Full-memory GPT-5-mini MCQ is reported as mean (range) across three case-order permutations (µ=0.79, σ=0.025). Dashes indicate incomplete runs. Best scores are marked in bold.
Figure 2: Cumulative MCQ accuracy over sequential benchmark streams. Left: GPT-5-mini under tool-free baselines and full memory. Right: Gemini-3-Flash under tool-enabled baseline and full memory.

Figure 3: Evo-MedAgent showcase across two chest X-ray cases. Earlier Failure: the agent incorrectly attributes a left pleural effusion to pneumonia; ground truth is haemothorax from a rib spur. Memory Evolution: episodic reflection identifies the missed bone review and distils two new procedural rules. Memory-guided Success: on a later case, the without-memory agent misdiagnoses right lower lobe opacification as atelectasis, while the memory-augmented agent applies the retrieved rules (meniscus sign, absence of air bronchograms) to correctly identify a malignant pleural-based mass.
